children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The park shall be administered in accordance with this subchapter and with the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including, but not limited to, the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; ).August 25, 191639 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1–41See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Donations
children.label: b Donations
children.text: The Secretary may accept donations of funds, property, or services from individuals, foundations, corporations, and other private entities, and from public entities, for the purposes of managing the park.

children.heading: Programs
children.label: c Programs
children.text: The Secretary may sponsor, coordinate, or enter into cooperative agreements for educational or cultural programs related to the park as the Secretary considers appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Identification and marking of significant historical sites
children.label: d Identification and marking of significant historical sites
children.text: The Secretary may identify other significant sites related to the Wright brothers, the history of aviation, or Paul Laurence Dunbar in the Miami Valley which are related to the park, and, with the consent of the owner or owners thereof, may mark the sites appropriately and make reference to them in any interpretive literature. The Secretary may provide interpretive markers along transportation routes leading to units of the park.

children.heading: Interpretation of Huffman Prairie Flying Field
children.label: e Interpretation of Huffman Prairie Flying Field
children.text: The Secretary may provide interpretation of Huffman Prairie Flying Field on Wright Brothers Hill, Wright-Patterson Air Force Base, Ohio.
